divergence: The Great Depression never occurred because money was abolished first
story_angle: breathless coverage of a trivial event treated as the hinge of history
headline: Reputation Exchange Closes at Record High as Traders Corner the Market on Being Delightful
deck: One skeptic asked what backs the Delightful Index and was socially bankrupted by lunch.
article:
NEW YORK — Champagne corks rang off the vaulted ceiling of the Reputation Exchange on Thursday as the Delightful Index closed up three hundred points — points of what, no one asks, as asking is bearish — capping the most effervescent quarter since the Abolition of Money made everyone rich in the only currency left: being liked.

The day's fireworks came courtesy of financier Bertram Vance, who successfully cornered the market on wit by placing every amusing dinner guest in Manhattan under exclusive contract. Rival houses scrambled to cover their positions in charm, sending the price of a well-timed compliment to heights not seen since the Whitney divorce. Margin calls were settled, as is custom, in favors, applause, and one very public apology performed in the lobby to a standing ovation, which the apologizer immediately banked.

The Federal Charm Reserve moved swiftly to steady the market, raising the polite-attention rate a quarter point. "Our fundamentals are sound," Chairwoman Evelyn Ashworth-Vane assured reporters, dazzlingly. "American charisma remains the envy of the civilized world, and envy, I remind you, is also up."

Not everyone toasted. At a luncheon downtown, a Mr. C. Howell of Gramercy Park inquired, aloud, what precisely backs the Delightful Index. The room fell silent. By dessert his club memberships had been called in; by evening his name was trading as a cautionary limerick; by Friday he was reachable only through his attorney, who asked that we describe him as "a former acquaintance." The Exchange has classified the incident as insider gloom.

Still, the omens glitter. A shoeshine boy outside the Exchange offered this reporter a tip — "compliments on hats are undervalued, mister" — and historians will note such moments precede either a golden age or a correction. The floor is untroubled. As one trader put it, raising his glass to the ticker: "The market can stay charming longer than you can stay solvent, and nobody's been solvent since '27. That, friend, is the genius of it."
author: Dorothy 'Dot' Marlowe, Society & Securities

oped.title: Charm Is the Only Currency That Never Inflates
oped.author: Mrs. Evelyn Ashworth-Vane, Chairwoman, Federal Charm Reserve (and Three Galas)
oped.body: The gloom-mongers — both of them, and you know their names, or rather you don't, which proves my point — insist our prosperity is 'a bubble of mutual flattery.' To which I say: and? Gold was a rock we agreed to adore. The dollar was paper with a haircut on it. At least charm does something; it gets you a table. Under my chairmanship the Reserve has kept polite attention flowing to every deserving household, and if certain dreary persons find themselves illiquid, perhaps they might try being more delightful, which is free, unless you need lessons, which are extensive. My critics call this circular. Of course it is circular, darlings. It's a dance.

classifieds.text: Will exchange summer villa, Oyster Bay, for one (1) devastating comeback, deliverable at the Whitney gala. Wit certified or deal is off.
Laughers wanted for the trading floor. Sincere laughter only — the Exchange can tell, and so, worse, can Mrs. Vance.
Formerly delightful gentleman, recently downgraded, seeks anyone still returning his calls. Excellent stories about the crash of my reputation. You'll laugh, which frankly I could use on my books.
Mr. C. Howell of Gramercy Park disputes his social bankruptcy. His creditors respond, in full: 'Who?'

panels.caption: Trader: 'Buy flattery! The Vanderbilt wedding is Saturday!'
The floor erupts in compliments. Three men faint from sheer delight and are commended for it.
A janitor asks what happens when everyone runs out of nice things to say. Trading halts. He's escorted out for insider gloom.

joke.setup: Why was the banker's smile trading at a premium?
joke.punchline: Fully backed by teeth and nothing else.
joke.text: Why was the banker's smile trading at a premium? Fully backed by teeth and nothing else.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Delightful Index Fund
sponsor_ads.body: Diversify across five hundred of the nation's most charming individuals. Past charisma is no guarantee of future charm.

sponsor_ads.headline: Vance & Vance Compliment Brokerage
sponsor_ads.body: We say it so you don't have to mean it.

sponsor_ads.headline: Champagne Airways
sponsor_ads.body: Fly above the Exchange and look down on everyone, aspirationally.

sponsor_ads.headline: Howell's Discount Sincerity
sponsor_ads.body: Honest opinions, going cheap. Going very cheap. Please.

real_world_echo: Meme stocks, crypto, and the influencer attention economy — a market where the asset is being liked and skepticism is a bannable offense.
